1→/* 
     2→ * @file   BQ24297.c
     3→ * @brief This file manages the BQ24297 module
     4→ */
     5→#define LOG_LVL LOG_LEVEL_BQ24297
     6→
     7→#include "BQ24297.h"
     8→#include "Util/Logger.h"
     9→#include "services/UsbCdc/UsbCdc.h"
    10→#include "peripheral/gpio/plib_gpio.h"
    11→
    12→// BQ24297 bypass for demo boards without BQ24297 populated
    13→// Uncomment the following line for boards without BQ24297 chip
    14→// #define BYPASS_BQ24297 1
    15→
    16→//! Pointer to the BQ24297 device configuration data structure
    17→static tBQ24297Config *pConfigBQ24;
    18→//! Pointer to the BQ24297 device "Write Variables" data structure
    19→static tBQ24297WriteVars *pWriteVariables;
    20→//! Pointer to the data structure where the BQ24297 data is
    21→static tBQ24297Data *pData;
    22→
    23→/*! Funtion to read BQ24297 data by I2C communication
    24→ * @param[in] reg Register to read
    25→ */
    26→uint8_t BQ24297_Read_I2C(uint8_t reg);
    27→
    28→/*! Funtion to write BQ24297 data by I2C communication
    29→ * @param[in] reg Register to read
    30→ * @param[in] txData Data to write
    31→ * @return true if write succeeded, false on error
    32→ */
    33→bool BQ24297_Write_I2C(uint8_t reg, uint8_t txData);
    34→
    35→void BQ24297_InitHardware(                                                  
    36→                        tBQ24297Config *pConfigInit,                        
    37→                        tBQ24297WriteVars *pWriteInit,                      
    38→                        tBQ24297Data *pDataInit) {
    39→    pConfigBQ24 = pConfigInit;
    40→    pWriteVariables = pWriteInit;
    41→    pData = pDataInit;
    42→
    43→#ifdef BYPASS_BQ24297
    44→    // Demo board bypass: Set up dummy "good" status values
    45→    LOG_I("BQ24297_InitHardware: BYPASS_BQ24297 enabled - simulating working BQ24297");
    46→    
    47→    // Mark as initialized
    48→    pData->initComplete = true;
    49→    pData->iinlimState = IINLIM_STATE_IDLE;
    50→    pData->iinlimLastVbus = false;
    51→
    52→    // Set "good" power status values for demo board
    53→    pData->status.pgStat = 1;        // External power present
    54→    pData->status.vsysStat = 0;      // Battery voltage good (>3.0V)
    55→    pData->status.batPresent = 1;    // Battery present
    56→    pData->status.chgStat = 3;       // Charge complete
    57→    pData->status.ntcFault = 0;      // NTC normal
    58→    pData->status.otg = 0;           // OTG disabled
    59→    pData->status.chgEn = 1;         // Charging enabled
    60→    pData->chargeAllowed = true;     // Allow charging
    61→    
    62→    LOG_I("BQ24297_InitHardware: Demo values - pgStat=%d, vsysStat=%d, batPresent=%d", 
    63→          pData->status.pgStat, pData->status.vsysStat, pData->status.batPresent);
    64→    
    65→    // Drive OTG LOW — DPDM will set 100mA (conservative).
    66→    // ManageIINLIM state machine overrides IINLIM via I2C after DPDM completes.
    67→    BATT_MAN_OTG_OutputEnable();
    68→    BATT_MAN_OTG_Clear();
    69→
    70→    LOG_I("BQ24297_InitHardware: Demo board initialized with good power status");
    71→    return;
    72→#endif
    73→    // Battery management initialization (hardware interface)
    74→
    75→    // ***Disable I2C calls*** as Harmony 2.06 doesn't have a working, 
    76→    // interrupt-safe implementation
    77→    // Open the I2C Driver for Master
    78→    pData->I2C_Handle = DRV_I2C_Open(                                       
    79→                        pConfigBQ24->I2C_Index,                             
    80→                        DRV_IO_INTENT_READWRITE|DRV_IO_INTENT_BLOCKING);
    81→
    82→    // Drive OTG LOW — DPDM will set 100mA (conservative).
    83→    // ManageIINLIM state machine overrides IINLIM via I2C after DPDM completes.
    84→    BATT_MAN_OTG_OutputEnable();
    85→    BATT_MAN_OTG_Clear();
    86→}
    87→
    88→void BQ24297_Config_Settings(void) {
    89→    // Temporary value to hold current register value
    90→    uint8_t reg = 0;
    91→    
    92→    LOG_D("BQ24297_Config_Settings: Starting initialization");
    93→    
    94→    // Read initial status to determine current power state
    95→    BQ24297_UpdateStatus();
    96→
    97→    // Read REG00 to preserve current limit settings
    98→    reg = BQ24297_Read_I2C(0x00);
    99→    
   100→    // Clear HIZ mode if set - it can cause power loss
   101→    if (reg & 0x80) {
   102→        LOG_D("BQ24297_Config_Settings: Clearing HIZ mode");
   103→    }
   104→
   105→    // Set input voltage limit to 3.88V (minimum): VINDPM = 0
   106→    // This gives maximum headroom before input voltage regulation kicks in
   107→    // REG00: [7]=0 (CLEAR HIZ), [6:3]=0000 (3.88V min), [2:0]=keep current limit
   108→    BQ24297_Write_I2C(0x00, reg & 0b00000111);  // This also clears HIZ bit
   109→
   110→    // Configure REG01 for charging mode
   111→    // REG01 bits:
   112→    // [7:6] = 01 (reset watchdog)
   113→    // [5] = 0 (OTG disable)
   114→    // [4] = 1 (CHARGE ENABLE)
   115→    // [3:1] = 000 (SYS_MIN = 3.0V)
   116→    // [0] = 1 (reserved)
   117→    
   118→    // Drive OTG LOW — DPDM sets 100mA (conservative), ManageIINLIM overrides via I2C
   119→    BATT_MAN_OTG_OutputEnable();
   120→    BATT_MAN_OTG_Clear();
   121→
   122→    // Configure for charge mode with SYS_MIN=3.0V to prevent BATFET disable
   123→    BQ24297_Write_I2C(0x01, 0b01010001);  // Watchdog reset, OTG=0, CHG=1, SYS_MIN=3.0V
   124→
   125→    // Set fast charge to 2000mA - this should never need to be updated
   126→    BQ24297_Write_I2C(0x02, 0b01100000);
   127→    
   128→    // REG03: Pre-charge and termination current
   129→    // Set minimum termination current (128mA) to avoid premature termination
   130→    // [7:5]=000 (128mA precharge), [4]=0 (reserved), [3:0]=0001 (128mA termination)
   131→    BQ24297_Write_I2C(0x03, 0b00000001);
   132→
   133→    // REG04: Charge voltage and battery thresholds
   134→    // [7:2]=100101 (4.096V), [1]=1 (3.0V battery low), [0]=0 (100mV recharge)
   135→    // Set BATLOWV to 3.0V (higher threshold) to keep BATFET enabled longer
   136→    BQ24297_Write_I2C(0x04, 0b10010110);
   137→
   138→    // REG05: Disable watchdog and maximize charge timer
   139→    // [7]=1 (enable termination), [6]=0 (disable timer), [5:4]=00 (disable watchdog)
   140→    // [3]=0 (normal), [2:1]=11 (20hr timer), [0]=0 (reserved)
   141→    // CRITICAL: Watchdog disabled to prevent unexpected BATFET disable
   142→    BQ24297_Write_I2C(0x05, 0b10001110);
   143→    
   144→    // REG06: Boost voltage and thermal regulation
   145→    // [7:4]=0111 (4.998V boost), [3:2]=00 (reserved), [1:0]=11 (120C thermal limit)
   146→    // Keep default boost voltage but set highest thermal limit
   147→    BQ24297_Write_I2C(0x06, 0b01110011);
   148→    
   149→    // Ensure BATFET is enabled (REG07 bit 5 = 0)
   150→    // Without BATFET, there's no path from battery to system
   151→    reg = BQ24297_Read_I2C(0x07);
   152→    if (reg & 0b00100000) {  // Check if bit 5 is set (BATFET disabled)
   153→        reg = reg & 0b11011111;  // Clear bit 5 to enable BATFET
   154→        BQ24297_Write_I2C(0x07, reg);
   155→        LOG_D("BQ24297: BATFET re-enabled");
   156→    }
   157→
   158→    // Read the current status
   159→    BQ24297_UpdateStatus();
   160→
   161→    // Start with conservative 500mA IINLIM
   162→    // BQ24297_ManageIINLIM() state machine will adjust based on USB enumeration
   163→    BQ24297_SetIINLIM(ILim_500);
   164→    pData->iinlimState = IINLIM_STATE_IDLE;
   165→    pData->iinlimTimestamp = 0;
   166→    pData->iinlimLastVbus = false;
   167→
   168→    // Update battery presence status (for reporting only)
   169→    BQ24297_UpdateBatteryStatus();
   170→
   171→    // Charging is always enabled via REG01 configuration above.
   172→    // The BQ24297 has built-in hardware protection for:
   173→    // - Battery temperature (NTC monitoring)
   174→    // - Overvoltage/overcurrent
   175→    // - Battery presence detection
   176→    // Let the hardware handle charging decisions autonomously.
   177→
   178→    // Mark initialization complete
   179→    pData->initComplete = true;
   180→

1→/* 
     2→ * File:   UsbCdc.h
     3→ * Author: Daniel
     4→ *
     5→ * Created on August 4, 2016, 3:24 PM
     6→ */
     7→
     8→#ifndef USBCDC_H
     9→#define	USBCDC_H
    10→
    11→#ifdef	__cplusplus
    12→extern "C" {
    13→#endif
    14→
    15→#include <stdint.h>
    16→#include <stdbool.h>
    17→#include <stddef.h>
    18→#include <stdlib.h>
    19→#include <string.h>
    20→#include <stdio.h>
    21→#include "configuration.h"
    22→#include "definitions.h"
    23→#include "libraries/microrl/src/microrl.h"
    24→#include "libraries/scpi/libscpi/inc/scpi/scpi.h"
    25→#include "Util/CircularBuffer.h"
    26→
    27→#define USBCDC_WBUFFER_SIZE 4096
    28→#define USBCDC_RBUFFER_SIZE 512
    29→#define USBCDC_CIRCULAR_BUFF_SIZE USBCDC_WBUFFER_SIZE*4
    30→
    31→// Command history for debugging
    32→#define SCPI_CMD_HISTORY_SIZE 10
    33→#define SCPI_CMD_MAX_LENGTH 128
    34→
    35→/**
    36→     * State machine states
    37→     */
    38→    typedef enum e_UsbCdcState {
    39→        /** Application's state machine's initial state. */
    40→        USB_CDC_STATE_INIT = 0,
    41→
    42→        /** Wait for a character receive */
    43→        USB_CDC_STATE_PROCESS,
    44→
    45→        /** Triggers the close process */
    46→        USB_CDC_STATE_BEGIN_CLOSE,
    47→
    48→        /** Waits for an event, not processing data */
    49→        USB_CDC_STATE_WAIT,
    50→
    51→        /** Called when the device is closed*/
    52→        USB_CDC_STATE_CLOSED
    53→
    54→    } UsbCdcState_t;
    55→
    56→    /**
    57→     * Contains the parameters for the CDC endpoint
    58→     */
    59→
    60→    typedef struct s_UsbCdcData {
    61→        /** Device layer handle returned by device layer open function */
    62→        USB_DEVICE_HANDLE deviceHandle;
    63→
    64→        /** Application's current state*/
    65→        UsbCdcState_t state;
    66→

    69→
    70→        /** Get Line Coding Data */

    72→
    73→        /** Control Line State */
    74→        USB_CDC_CONTROL_LINE_STATE controlLineStateData;
    75→
    76→        /** Read transfer handle */
    77→        USB_DEVICE_CDC_TRANSFER_HANDLE readTransferHandle;
    78→
    79→        /** Write transfer handle */
    80→        USB_DEVICE_CDC_TRANSFER_HANDLE writeTransferHandle;
    81→
    82→        /** Break data */

    93→

    96→
    97→        /** Client read buffer */
    98→        uint8_t readBuffer[USBCDC_RBUFFER_SIZE] __attribute__((coherent, aligned(16)));
    99→        ;
   100→
   101→        /** Client write buffer */
   102→        uint8_t writeBuffer[USBCDC_WBUFFER_SIZE] __attribute__((coherent, aligned(16)));
   103→
   104→        CircularBuf_t wCirbuf;
   105→        SemaphoreHandle_t wMutex;
   106→
   107→        /** The flag is if the CDC is in transparent mode
   108→         ie the received data wont go to microrl interface
   109→         so no scip commands would work*/
   110→        bool isCdcHostConnected;
   111→        bool isTransparentModeActive;
   112→        
   113→        /** USB VBUS detection from microcontroller (independent of BQ24297) */
   114→        bool isVbusDetected;
   115→
   116→        /** USB host completed enumeration (SET_CONFIGURATION) */
   117→        bool isConfigured;
   118→

   120→        char cmdHistory[SCPI_CMD_HISTORY_SIZE][SCPI_CMD_MAX_LENGTH];
   121→        uint8_t cmdHistoryHead;  // Next write position
   122→        uint8_t cmdHistoryCount; // Number of commands stored
   123→        
   124→        /** Escape sequence parsing state for input filtering */
   125→        enum {
   126→            ESC_STATE_NONE = 0, // Normal input
   127→            ESC_STATE_ESC,      // Received ESC

   135→    void UsbCdc_Initialize();

   145→    bool UsbCdc_IsActive();

   147→    /**

   154→    size_t UsbCdc_WriteToBuffer(UsbCdcData_t* client, const char* data, size_t len);
   155→
   156→    /**

   162→    size_t UsbCdc_WriteDefault(const char* data, size_t len);
   163→

   170→    void UsbCdc_SetTransparentMode(bool value);
   171→    
   172→    /**

   177→    bool UsbCdc_IsVbusDetected(void);

   182→     */
   183→    bool UsbCdc_IsConfigured(void);
   184→

   199→    tRunTimeUsbSettings* UsbCdc_GetRuntimeSettings(void);

content.text:
Read CLAUDE.md and check if any sections need updating based on the changes in this PR (git diff main..HEAD). The PR introduces:

1. Timer-based IINLIM state machine (replaces old DPDM-dependent approach)
2. OTG pin driven LOW (was previously set as input with hardware pullup)
3. POWER_DETECTED delay reduced from 1000ms to 100ms
4. New SCPI diagnostic commands: SYST:POW:BQ:REG?, SYST:POW:BQ:ILIM, SYST:POW:BQ:DPDM, SYST:POW:BQ:DIAG?
5. scpi_printf helper added
6. UsbCdc_IsConfigured() tracking
7. BQ24297_SetIINLIM() and BQ24297_ManageIINLIM() new functions

Check these CLAUDE.md sections specifically:
- "BQ24297 Register Init (Config_Settings)" section — does it mention IINLIM or OTG changes?
- "OTG Pin and DPDM Detection" section — this is in MEMORY.md, but check if CLAUDE.md references the old approach
- Any SCPI command tables that should list the new diagnostic commands
- Architecture/component hierarchy that should mention IINLIM management
- Any timing references (1000ms delays, etc.) that are now stale

Read: CLAUDE.md, and also check /home/user/.claude/projects/-mnt-c-Users-User-Documents-GitHub-daqifi-nyquist-firmware/memory/MEMORY.md

Report what needs updating.

1→# CLAUDE.md
     2→
     3→This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4→
     5→## Project Overview
     6→
     7→This is the DAQiFi Nyquist firmware project - a multi-channel data acquisition device built on PIC32MZ microcontroller with FreeRTOS. The project consists of a USB bootloader and main application firmware.
     8→
     9→## Build Instructions
    10→
    11→### Prerequisites
    12→- MPLAB X IDE (Microchip's development environment)
    13→- XC32 Compiler for PIC32
    14→- Harmony Configurator (MHC) for code generation
    15→
    16→### Building Firmware Only
    17→1. Open `firmware/daqifi.X` project in MPLAB X
    18→2. In Project Properties, exclude all linker files from the build
    19→3. Build and flash directly to the device
    20→
    21→### Building Bootloader-Compatible Firmware
    22→1. Open `firmware/daqifi.X` project in MPLAB X
    23→2. In Project Properties, include `old_hv2_bootld.ld` linker script
    24→3. Build the firmware
    25→4. Flash the bootloader first: `bootloader/firmware/usb_bootloader.X`
    26→5. Then flash the firmware using the Windows DAQiFi application or include it in bootloader Project Properties → Loading menu
    27→
    28→### Building from Command Line (Windows with WSL/Linux)
    29→1. Ensure MPLAB X is installed (typically in `C:\Program Files\Microchip\MPLABX`)
    30→2. Clean build artifacts: `rm -rf firmware/daqifi.X/build/ dist/`
    31→3. Build using MPLAB X's make utility:
    32→   ```bash
    33→   cd firmware/daqifi.X
    34→   "/mnt/c/Program Files/Microchip/MPLABX/v6.25/gnuBins/GnuWin32/bin/make.exe" -f nbproject/Makefile-default.mk CONF=default build -j$(nproc)
    35→   ```
    36→   Note: `-j$(nproc)` uses all available CPU cores for faster builds
    37→4. The output hex file will be in `dist/default/production/daqifi.X.production.hex` (approx 1MB)
    38→
    39→Note: XC32 compiler is also available in Linux at `/opt/microchip/xc32/v4.60/bin/xc32-gcc`
    40→
    41→### Programming with PICkit 4 from Command Line
    42→1. Connect PICkit 4 to the device
    43→2. Use ipecmd to program the hex file:
    44→   ```bash
    45→   cd firmware/daqifi.X
    46→   "/mnt/c/Program Files/Microchip/MPLABX/v6.25/mplab_platform/mplab_ipe/ipecmd.exe" \
    47→     -TPPK4 -P32MZ2048EFM144 -M -F"dist/default/production/daqifi.X.production.hex" -OL
    48→   ```
    49→3. The device will be erased and programmed automatically
    50→4. Look for "Program Succeeded" message
    51→
    52→Command options:
    53→- `-TPPK4`: Use PICkit 4 as programmer
    54→- `-P32MZ2048EFM144`: Target device
    55→- `-M`: Program mode
    56→- `-F`: Hex file to program
    57→- `-OL`: Use loaded memories only
    58→
    59→### Bootloader Entry
    60→- Hold the user button for ~20 seconds until board resets
    61→- Release button when LEDs light solid
    62→- Hold button again until white LED blinks to enter bootloader mode
    63→
    64→## Architecture Overview
    65→
    66→### Component Hierarchy
    67→```
    68→Main Application (FreeRTOS Tasks)
    69→├── USB Device Task (app_USBDeviceTask)
    70→├── WiFi Task (app_WifiTask)
    71→├── SD Card Task (app_SdCardTask)
    72→├── Streaming Task
    73→└── SCPI Command Processing
    74→    
    75→Hardware Abstraction Layer (HAL)
    76→├── ADC (AD7173, AD7609, MC12bADC)
    77→├── DAC (DAC7718)
    78→├── DIO (Digital I/O)
    79→├── Power Management (BQ24297)
    80→└── UI (User Interface/LEDs)
    81→
    82→Services Layer
    83→├── Data Encoding (JSON, CSV, Protocol Buffers)
    84→├── Communication (USB CDC, WiFi TCP Server, SCPI)
    85→├── Storage (SD Card Manager)
    86→└── Streaming Engine
    87→```
    88→
    89→### Key Data Structures
    90→
    91→1. **Board Configuration** (`tBoardConfig` in `state/board/BoardConfig.h`)
    92→   - Immutable hardware configuration
    93→   - Defines available channels, modules, and capabilities
    94→   - Three variants: NQ1, NQ2, NQ3
    95→
    96→2. **Board Runtime Configuration** (`tBoardRuntimeConfig` in `state/runtime/BoardRuntimeConfig.h`)
    97→   - Mutable runtime settings
    98→   - Channel configurations, sampling rates, triggers
    99→
   100→3. **Board Data** (`tBoardData` in `state/data/BoardData.h`)
   101→   - Real-time acquisition data
   102→   - Sample buffers and timestamps
   103→
   104→### Communication Protocols
   105→
   106→1. **SCPI Commands** - Standard Commands for Programmable Instruments
   107→   - Entry point: `services/SCPI/SCPIInterface.c`
   108→   - Modules: SCPIADC, SCPIDIO, SCPILAN, SCPIStorageSD
   109→   - Used for device configuration and control
   110→   - **SCPI Abbreviation Rule**: Commands can be abbreviated based on the capitalization in the full command. The abbreviated command must contain all letters that are in CAPS. For example:
   111→     - `SYST:COMM:LAN:NETMode` can be abbreviated as `SYST:COMM:LAN:NETM`
   112→     - `SYST:COMM:LAN:APPLy` can be abbreviated as `SYST:COMM:LAN:APPL`
   113→
   114→#### SCPI Command Verification Protocol
   115→
   116→**⚠️ CRITICAL: NEVER guess SCPI command syntax. ALWAYS verify first.**
   117→
   118→**Mandatory Process Before Using ANY SCPI Command:**
   119→
   120→1. **Search for the exact pattern** in `SCPIInterface.c`:
   121→   ```bash
   122→   grep -n "pattern.*<keyword>" firmware/src/services/SCPI/SCPIInterface.c
   123→   ```
   124→
   125→2. **Use the EXACT command** from the pattern definition - do not abbreviate unless testing abbreviations
   126→
   127→3. **Verify parameter syntax** by reading the callback function implementation
   128→
   129→**Common Mistakes to Avoid:**
   130→- ❌ `SYST:STAR 5000` (guessed)
   131→- ✅ `SYST:StartStreamData 5000` (verified from SCPIInterface.c:1521)
   132→
   133→- ❌ `SYST:STOP` (guessed)
   134→- ✅ `SYST:StopStreamData` (verified from SCPIInterface.c:1522)
   135→
   136→- ❌ `SYST:STOR:SD:GET DAQiFi/file.csv` (unquoted path)
   137→- ✅ `SYST:STOR:SD:GET "file.csv"` (quoted, verified from SCPIStorageSD.c)
   138→
   139→**Verification Example:**
   140→```bash
   141→# Step 1: Search for streaming commands
   142→grep -n "pattern.*Stream" firmware/src/services/SCPI/SCPIInterface.c
   143→# Result: Line 1521: {.pattern = "SYSTem:StartStreamData", .callback = SCPI_StartStreaming,},
   144→
   145→# Step 2: Use exact command
   146→device._comm.send_command("SYST:StartStreamData 5000")  # Correct!
   147→```
   148→
   149→**If you use a SCPI command without verifying it first, STOP immediately and verify the syntax.**
   150→
   151→2. **USB CDC** - Virtual COM port communication
   152→   - Implementation: `services/UsbCdc/UsbCdc.c`
   153→   - Handles command processing and data streaming
   154→
   155→3. **WiFi** - Network communication
   156→   - Manager: `services/wifi_services/wifi_manager.c`
   157→   - TCP server for remote control
   158→   - UDP announcements for device discovery
   159→
   160→### Data Flow
   161→
   162→1. **Acquisition Path**:
   163→   - Hardware interrupts → HAL drivers → Sample buffers
   164→   - ADC modules use DMA for high-speed acquisition
   165→   - DIO uses interrupt-driven capture
   166→
   167→2. **Streaming Path**:
   168→   - Sample buffers → Encoder (JSON/CSV/ProtoBuf) → Output interface (USB/WiFi/SD)
   169→   - Streaming engine manages buffer flow and encoding
   170→   - Supports multiple simultaneous outputs
   171→
   172→### Board Variants
   173→
   174→- **NQ1**: Basic variant configuration
   175→- **NQ2**: Enhanced variant with additional features
   176→- **NQ3**: Full-featured variant
   177→
   178→Each variant has specific:
   179→- Channel counts and types
   180→- ADC/DAC configurations
   181→- Power management settings
   182→- UI configurations
   183→
   184→#### Switching Between Board Variants
   185→
   186→The firmware supports building for different board variants using MPLAB X configurations:
   187→
   188→1. **In MPLAB X IDE**:
   189→   - Right-click project → Set Configuration → Select "Nq1" or "Nq3"
   190→   - Build the project
   191→
   192→2. **From Command Line**:
   193→   ```bash
   194→   # Build NQ1 firmware
   195→   "/mnt/c/Program Files/Microchip/MPLABX/v6.25/gnuBins/GnuWin32/bin/make.exe" \
   196→     -f nbproject/Makefile-Nq1.mk CONF=Nq1 build -j4
   197→
   198→   # Build NQ3 firmware
   199→   "/mnt/c/Program Files/Microchip/MPLABX/v6.25/gnuBins/GnuWin32/bin/make.exe" \
   200→     -f nbproject/Makefile-Nq3.mk CONF=Nq3 build -j4
   201→   ```
   202→
   203→3. **How It Works**:
   204→   - Each configuration includes/excludes variant-specific files
   205→   - `NqBoardConfig_Get()` returns the appropriate board configuration
   206→   - `NqBoardRuntimeConfig_GetDefaults()` returns the appropriate runtime defaults
   207→   - No code changes needed - just switch configuration and rebuild
   208→
   209→4. **Variant-Specific Files**:
   210→   - **NQ1**: `NQ1BoardConfig.c`, `NQ1RuntimeDefaults.c`
   211→   - **NQ3**: `NQ3BoardConfig.c`, `NQ3RuntimeDefaults.c`
   212→
   213→### Memory Considerations
   214→
   215→#### Heap Configuration
   216→- **Total FreeRTOS Heap**: 284KB (`configTOTAL_HEAP_SIZE` in `FreeRTOSConfig.h`)
   217→- **Heap Implementation**: heap_4 (best-fit with coalescence)
   218→- **Critical**: Heap must accommodate circular buffers, task stacks, and streaming sample queue
   219→
   220→#### Major Heap Allocations
   221→
   222→**Static Buffers** (compile-time allocation, not on heap):
   223→- **SD Card Shared Buffer**: 32KB (`gSdSharedBuffer` in `sd_card_manager.c`)
   224→  - Static coherent buffer for all SD operations (write, read, list)
   225→  - DMA-safe, cache-line aligned
   226→  - Reduced from 64KB to 32KB to allow larger sample pool
   227→  - Location: `firmware/src/services/sd_card_services/sd_card_manager.c`
   228→- **Sample Pool**: ~146KB (700 samples × ~210 bytes)
   229→  - Static pool for streaming analog input samples
   230→  - Uses O(1) free-list allocation to avoid heap fragmentation
   231→  - Location: `firmware/src/state/data/AInSample.c`
   232→
   233→**Circular Buffers** (heap-allocated at runtime):
   234→- **WiFi TCP Write Buffer**: 5.6KB (`WIFI_CIRCULAR_BUFF_SIZE` = 1400 × 4)
   235→  - Location: `firmware/src/services/wifi_services/wifi_tcp_server.c`
   236→- **USB CDC Write Buffer**: 16KB (`USBCDC_CIRCULAR_BUFF_SIZE` = 4096 × 4)
   237→  - Location: `firmware/src/services/UsbCdc/UsbCdc.c`
   238→  - Increased from 8KB to improve throughput and reduce buffer-full conditions
   239→
   240→**FreeRTOS Task Stacks** (~33KB total):
   241→- `app_SdCardTask`: 5240 bytes
   242→- `app_PowerAndUITask`: 4096 bytes
   243→- `streaming_Task`: 4096 bytes
   244→- `_Streaming_Deferred_Interrupt_Task`: 4096 bytes
   245→- `app_USBDeviceTask`: 3072 bytes (note: malloc fails with 4096)
   246→- `app_WifiTask`: 3000 bytes
   247→- `lWDRV_WINC_Tasks`: 3000 bytes (WiFi driver)
   248→- `MC12bADC_EosInterruptTask`: 2048 bytes
   249→- `lAPP_FREERTOS_Tasks`: 1500 bytes
   250→- `F_USB_DEVICE_Tasks`: 1024 bytes
   251→- `F_DRV_USBHS_Tasks`: 1024 bytes
   252→- `AD7609_DeferredInterruptTask`: 512 bytes
   253→
   254→**Streaming Sample Queue**:
   255→- **Pool Size**: 700 samples (`MAX_AIN_SAMPLE_COUNT` in `AInSample.h`)
   256→- **Sample Size**: ~208 bytes (`AInPublicSampleList_t` = 16 channels × 12 bytes + 16 bools)
   257→- **Total Pool Memory**: ~146KB (700 × 208 bytes, statically allocated)
   258→- **Allocation**: Static pool with O(1) free-list allocation (no heap usage)
   259→- **Critical**: Old samples must be freed before starting new streaming session
   260→  - Cleared automatically in `Streaming_Start()`
   261→
   262→#### Heap Usage Summary
   263→Typical allocations at steady state:
   264→- Circular buffers: **21.6KB** (WiFi: 5.6KB, USB: 16KB)
   265→- Task stacks: **~33KB**
   266→- **Total heap**: ~55KB used, **~229KB free** (under normal conditions)
   267→- **Static buffers**: ~178KB (32KB SD buffer + 146KB sample pool - not on heap)
   268→
   269→#### Memory Pressure Indicators
   270→If heap allocation fails (`xPortGetFreeHeapSize()` returns low values):
   271→1. **Check streaming sample pool**: `AInSampleList_Size()` - should be <700
   272→2. **Reduce USB/WiFi buffers**: If SD logging is primary use case, can reduce USB/WiFi buffer sizes
   273→3. **Monitor with debugger**: Set breakpoint in `pvPortMalloc` failure path
   274→
   275→Note: SD buffer and sample pool are now static (not heap), significantly improving heap availability.
   276→
   277→#### Other Memory Constraints
   278→- DMA buffers must be cache-aligned
   279→- Bootloader reserves memory at 0x9D000000
   280→- Application starts at 0x9D000480 (after bootloader)
   281→- Sample buffers use coherent attribute for DMA safety
   282→
   283→### Key Files for Understanding the System
   284→
   285→1. **Application Entry**: `firmware/src/app_freertos.c` - Main application tasks
   286→2. **Board Config**: `firmware/src/state/board/BoardConfig.c` - Hardware definitions
   287→3. **Streaming Engine**: `firmware/src/services/streaming.c` - Data flow control
   288→4. **SCPI Interface**: `firmware/src/services/SCPI/SCPIInterface.c` - Command processing
   289→5. **HAL Drivers**: `firmware/src/HAL/ADC.c`, `DIO.c`, `DAC7718/DAC7718.c` - Hardware interfaces
   290→
   291→### Development Considerations
   292→
   293→- All hardware access must go through HAL layer
   294→- SCPI commands follow IEEE 488.2 standard
   295→- Use FreeRTOS primitives for synchronization
   296→- Respect board variant differences in runtime checks
   297→- Protocol buffer definitions in `services/DaqifiPB/DaqifiOutMessage.proto`
   298→
   299→### DAC7718 Integration (NQ3 Board)
   300→
   301→The NQ3 board variant includes a DAC7718 8-channel 12-bit DAC for analog output functionality:
   302→
   303→#### Hardware Configuration
   304→- **SPI Interface**: SPI2 at 10 MHz (configurable)
   305→- **Control Pins**: 
   306→  - CS (Chip Select): RK0 (GPIO_PIN_RK0)
   307→  - CLR/RST (Reset): RJ13 (GPIO_PIN_RJ13) 
   308→  - LDAC: Tied to 3.3V (no GPIO control)
   309→- **Power Requirements**: Requires 10V rail (available in POWERED_UP state)
   310→- **Channels**: 8 analog output channels (0-7)
   311→- **Resolution**: 12-bit (4096 levels)
   312→
   313→#### SCPI Commands for DAC Control
   314→```bash
   315→# Set DAC channel voltage
   316→SOURce:VOLTage:LEVel 0,5.0        # Set channel 0 to 5.0V
   317→SOURce:VOLTage:LEVel 5.0          # Set all channels to 5.0V
   318→
   319→# Read DAC channel voltage  
   320→SOURce:VOLTage:LEVel? 0           # Read channel 0 voltage
   321→SOURce:VOLTage:LEVel?             # Read all channel voltages
   322→
   323→# Channel enable/disable
   324→ENAble:SOURce:DC 0,1              # Enable channel 0
   325→ENAble:SOURce:DC? 0               # Get channel 0 enable status
   326→
   327→# Configuration commands
   328→CONFigure:DAC:RANGe 0,1           # Set channel 0 range
   329→CONFigure:DAC:UPDATE              # Update all DAC outputs
   330→
   331→# Calibration commands
   332→CONFigure:DAC:chanCALM 0,1.0      # Set channel 0 calibration slope
   333→CONFigure:DAC:chanCALB 0,0.0      # Set channel 0 calibration offset
   334→CONFigure:DAC:SAVEcal             # Save user calibration values
   335→CONFigure:DAC:LOADcal             # Load user calibration values
   336→```
   337→
   338→#### Integration Points
   339→- **Board Configuration**: `state/board/AOutConfig.h` - Analog output configuration structure
   340→- **SCPI Module**: `services/SCPI/SCPIDAC.c` - DAC command implementation  
   341→- **HAL Driver**: `HAL/DAC7718/DAC7718.c` - Hardware abstraction layer
   342→- **Runtime Config**: `state/runtime/AOutRuntimeConfig.h` - Runtime channel states
   343→
   344→#### Power-Safe Initialization
   345→The DAC7718 uses lazy initialization that:
   346→1. Initializes global structures at startup (power-independent)
   347→2. Defers hardware initialization until first DAC command when power is sufficient
   348→3. Checks for POWERED_UP state (provides 10V rail) before hardware access
   349→4. Gracefully handles power state transitions
   350→
   351→## Firmware Analysis Report
   352→
   353→A comprehensive technical analysis of the firmware has been completed and is available in `FIRMWARE_ANALYSIS_REPORT.md`. This report provides:
   354→
   355→### Critical Findings
   356→- **Performance Bottleneck**: 700-byte buffer limitation constraining all communication channels
   357→- **Safety Risk**: Shared SPI bus between WiFi and SD card without mutex protection
   358→- **Architecture Issues**: Flat task priority structure causing scheduling inefficiencies
   359→- **Memory Management**: Dynamic allocation in real-time streaming paths
   360→
   361→### Key Recommendations
   362→1. **IMMEDIATE**: Implement SPI bus mutex protection
   363→2. **IMMEDIATE**: Replace shared buffer with per-channel buffers
   364→3. **HIGH**: Restructure FreeRTOS task priorities for real-time performance
   365→4. **HIGH**: Implement UDP streaming for high-throughput applications
   366→5. **MEDIUM**: Optimize memory management and eliminate dynamic allocation from streaming
   367→
   368→### Performance Projections
   369→- **Current WiFi**: ~5-10 Mbps → **Optimized**: 50-80 Mbps
   370→- **Current SD**: ~2-5 MB/s → **Optimized**: 15-25 MB/s
   371→- **Current ADC**: ~1 kHz → **Optimized**: 10+ kHz sample rates
   372→
   373→The report includes detailed technical analysis, code examples, and a prioritized roadmap for optimization. Reference this document before making performance-critical modifications to understand current limitations and optimization opportunities.
   374→
   375→### SD Card File Splitting
   376→
   377→**Feature:** Automatic file splitting prevents FAT32 4GB file size limit issues during long logging sessions.
   378→
   379→**Configuration:**
   380→```bash
   381→# Set maximum file size (range: 1000 bytes to 4GB)
   382→SYST:STOR:SD:MAXSize 2000000000    # 2GB limit
   383→SYST:STOR:SD:MAXSize 0              # Use filesystem maximum (3.9GB default)
   384→SYST:STOR:SD:MAXSize?               # Query current setting
   385→```
   386→
   387→**File Naming:**
   388→- First file: Uses original name (e.g., `experiment.csv`)
   389→- Split files: Sequential 4-digit numbering (e.g., `experiment-0001.csv`, `experiment-0002.csv`)
   390→- Supports up to 9999 files per session (~39TB @ 3.9GB each)
   391→
   392→**CSV Headers:**
   393→- First file: Full CSV header (device info, column names with "ain" prefix)
   394→- Split files: Data rows only (no headers) - simplifies merging
   395→
   396→**Safety Features:**
   397→- Default: 3.9GB limit (100MB below FAT32 maximum)
   398→- Minimum: 1000 bytes (prevents rapid rotation)
   399→- Circular buffer draining before rotation (zero data loss)
   400→- Unconditional filesystem flush before file close
   401→
   402→**Python Tools:**
   403→- `download_sd_files.py` - Auto-detects and groups split files
   404→- `analyze_split_files.py` - Validates integrity, merges parts
   405→
   406→**Implementation:** `firmware/src/services/sd_card_services/sd_card_manager.c`
   407→
   408→### Logging System
   409→
   410→The firmware includes a compile-time configurable logging system with per-module log level control. Logs are stored in a circular buffer and can be retrieved via SCPI commands.
   411→
   412→**Log Levels:**
   413→
   414→| Level | Macro | Value | Use Case |
   415→|-------|-------|-------|----------|
   416→| NONE | `LOG_LEVEL_NONE` | 0 | Disable all logging for a module |
   417→| ERROR | `LOG_LEVEL_ERROR` | 1 | Unexpected failures, hardware errors, critical issues |
   418→| INFO | `LOG_LEVEL_INFO` | 2 | State changes, significant events (connection, mode changes) |
   419→| DEBUG | `LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG` | 3 | Verbose diagnostics, data flow tracing, development |
   420→
   421→**Production Default:** All modules default to `LOG_LEVEL_ERROR`. Only `LOG_E()` calls produce output; `LOG_I()` and `LOG_D()` compile to nothing (zero runtime cost).
   422→
   423→**SCPI Commands:**
   424→```bash
   425→SYST:LOG?          # Retrieve all log messages (clears buffer after dump)
   426→SYST:LOG:CLEAR     # Clear log buffer without reading
   427→SYST:LOG:TEST      # Add test messages (for verification)
   428→```
   429→
   430→**Circular Buffer Behavior:**
   431→- Capacity: 64 messages, 128 bytes each
   432→- When full: Oldest message is dropped to make room for new
   433→- Thread-safe: Protected by FreeRTOS mutex
   434→- ISR-safe: Detects ISR context and skips buffering (prevents deadlock)
   435→
   436→**Enabling Verbose Logging (Development Only):**
   437→
   438→**IMPORTANT: Always reset all module log levels back to `LOG_LEVEL_ERROR` in `Logger.h` before release builds.** Debug/info logging adds overhead and fills the circular buffer, masking real errors in production.
   439→
   440→To enable INFO or DEBUG logging for a specific module, change its level in `Logger.h`:
   441→```c
   442→// Change from:
   443→#define LOG_LEVEL_WIFI      LOG_LEVEL_ERROR
   444→
   445→// To (for debugging):
   446→#define LOG_LEVEL_WIFI      LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG
   447→```
   448→
   449→Or define in the module's .c file before including Logger.h:
   450→```c
   451→#define LOG_LVL LOG_LEVEL_DEBUG  // Enable all logging for this file
   452→#include "Util/Logger.h"
   453→```
   454→
   455→**Available Module Log Levels:**
   456→- `LOG_LEVEL_POWER` - Power management (BQ24297, state transitions)
   457→- `LOG_LEVEL_WIFI` - WiFi manager, TCP server, UDP discovery
   458→- `LOG_LEVEL_BQ24297` - Battery charger IC
   459→- `LOG_LEVEL_SD` - SD card operations
   460→- `LOG_LEVEL_USB` - USB CDC interface
   461→- `LOG_LEVEL_SCPI` - SCPI command processing
   462→- `LOG_LEVEL_ADC` - ADC drivers (AD7609, MC12bADC)
   463→- `LOG_LEVEL_DAC` - DAC7718 driver
   464→
   465→**Real-Time UART Logging (Development Only):**
   466→
   467→For live debugging without USB, enable ICSP UART output:
   468→```c
   469→// In project defines or Logger.h:
   470→#define ENABLE_ICSP_REALTIME_LOG 1
   471→```
   472→This outputs logs via UART4 on ICSP pin 4 (RB0) at 921600 baud. Must be disabled before release.
   473→
   474→**Implementation:** `firmware/src/Util/Logger.c`, `firmware/src/Util/Logger.h`
   475→
   476→## Development Notes
   477→
   478→### Building with Linux/WSL
   479→
   480→The project can be built using Microchip tools in WSL/Linux:
   481→
   482→#### Prerequisites in WSL

   487→
   488→#### Building from Command Line

   492→   ```

   497→   ```
   498→
   499→3. Clean build directories (if previous build failed):
   500→   ```bash
   501→   rm -rf build dist
   502→   ```
   503→
   504→4. Build the project:
   505→   ```bash
   506→   make -f nbproject/Makefile-default.mk CONF=default build -j4
   507→   ```
   508→
   509→5. Output hex file location:
   510→   ```
   511→   dist/default/production/daqifi.X.production.hex (approx 1MB)
   512→   ```
   513→
   514→#### Programming with PICkit 4
   515→
   516→##### From WSL (Verified Working Method)
   517→```bash
   518→"/mnt/c/Program Files/Microchip/MPLABX/v6.25/mplab_platform/mplab_ipe/ipecmd.exe" \
   519→  -TPPK4 -P32MZ2048EFM144 -M -F"dist/default/production/daqifi.X.production.hex" -OL
   520→```
   521→
   522→##### From Windows PowerShell or Command Prompt
   523→1. Navigate to project directory:
   524→   ```cmd
   525→   cd C:\Users\User\Documents\GitHub\daqifi-nyquist-firmware\firmware\daqifi.X
   526→   ```
   527→
   528→2. Program the device:
   529→   ```cmd
   530→   C:\"Program Files"\Microchip\MPLABX\v6.25\mplab_platform\mplab_ipe\ipecmd.exe -TPPK4 -P32MZ2048EFM144 -M -F"dist\default\production\daqifi.X.production.hex" -OL
   531→   ```
   532→
   533→**Important**: The `-OL` flag is required for successful programming
   534→
   535→2. For Linux builds, the hex file will be at the WSL path:
   536→   ```
   537→   \\wsl$\Ubuntu\mnt\c\Users\User\Documents\GitHub\daqifi-nyquist-firmware\firmware\daqifi.X\dist\default\production\daqifi.X.production.hex
   538→   ```
   539→
   540→### Device Testing and SCPI Communication
   541→
   542→#### Important Testing Notes
   543→1. **Picocom Usage**: Never use `2>&1` with picocom commands - it doesn't handle stderr redirection properly
   544→   ```bash
   545→   # Good: 
   546→   (echo -e "*IDN?\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 | tail -5
   547→   # Bad:
   548→   (echo -e "*IDN?\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 2>&1 | tail -5
   549→   ```
   550→
   551→2. **Power State Required**: Always power up the device before attempting WiFi or DAC operations
   552→   ```bash
   553→   # Check power state (0=STANDBY, 1=POWERED_UP, 2=POWERED_UP_EXT_DOWN)
   554→   (echo -e "SYST:POW:STAT?\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 | tail -5
   555→   
   556→   # Power up device for full functionality (enables 10V rail needed for DAC7718)
   557→   (echo -e "SYST:POW:STAT 1\r"; sleep 1) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 | tail-5
   558→   ```
   559→
   560→3. **Use Exact SCPI Commands**: Don't guess command syntax. Check the actual command table in `SCPIInterface.c`
   561→   ```bash
   562→   # Correct commands (from SCPIInterface.c):
   563→   SYST:COMM:LAN:SSIDSTR?    # Signal strength (not SSID:STR)
   564→   SYST:COMM:LAN:NETTYPE?     # Network type (not MODE or NETWORK)
   565→   SYST:COMM:LAN:ADDR?        # IP address (not ADDRESS or IP)
   566→   ```
   567→
   568→#### USB Device Access from WSL
   569→1. **Initial device attachment** (from Windows PowerShell as admin):
   570→   ```powershell
   571→   usbipd list
   572→   # Find DAQiFi device (e.g., "USB Serial Device (COM3)")
   573→   usbipd attach --wsl --busid <BUSID>
   574→   ```
   575→   Note: May require `--force` if USBPcap filter is installed
   576→
   577→2. **Reconnecting device from WSL side** (after PC sleep or device reprogramming):
   578→   ```bash
   579→   # List USB devices to find DAQiFi
   580→   powershell.exe -Command "usbipd list"
   581→   # Look for: 2-4    04d8:f794  USB Serial Device (COM3)
   582→   
   583→   # Attach from WSL
   584→   powershell.exe -Command "usbipd attach --wsl --busid 2-4"
   585→   
   586→   # Wait for device to appear
   587→   sleep 2 && ls -la /dev/ttyACM*
   588→   ```
   589→
   590→3. **Verify device in WSL**:
   591→   ```bash
   592→   lsusb | grep -i "04d8"
   593→   # Should show: Microchip Technology, Inc. Nyquist
   594→   
   595→   ls -la /dev/ttyACM*
   596→   # Should show: /dev/ttyACM0
   597→   ```
   598→
   599→#### Sending SCPI Commands
   600→Use picocom for reliable serial communication:
   601→```bash
   602→# Query device identification
   603→(echo -e "*IDN?\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 2>&1 | tail -20
   604→
   605→# Common SCPI commands:
   606→# WiFi configuration
   607→(echo -e "SYST:COMM:LAN:SSID?\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 2>&1 | tail -20
   608→(echo -e "SYST:COMM:LAN:SSID \"NetworkName\"\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 2>&1 | tail -20
   609→
   610→# Power management
   611→(echo -e "SYST:POW:STAT?\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 2>&1 | tail -20
   612→(echo -e "SYST:POW:STAT 2\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 2>&1 | tail -20
   613→```
   614→
   615→Power states:
   616→- 0: STANDBY (MCU on if USB powered, off if battery powered)
   617→- 1: POWERED_UP (Board fully powered, all modules active)
   618→- 2: POWERED_UP_EXT_DOWN (Partial power, external power disabled - low battery mode)
   619→
   620→#### Windows Network Access
   621→Check WiFi networks from WSL using Windows netsh:
   622→```bash
   623→# List all visible WiFi networks
   624→powershell.exe -Command "netsh wlan show networks"
   625→
   626→# Check current WiFi interface status
   627→powershell.exe -Command "netsh wlan show interfaces"
   628→```
   629→
   630→#### DAQiFi WiFi Access Point
   631→When powered up, the device creates an open WiFi access point:
   632→- Default SSID: "DAQiFi"
   633→- Authentication: Open (no password)
   634→- The device must be in POWERED_UP state for WiFi to operate
   635→
   636→### Automated Testing Notes
   637→
   638→#### Quick Device Recovery Script
   639→Create a script to quickly reconnect the device:
   640→```bash
   641→#!/bin/bash
   642→# reconnect_device.sh
   643→echo "Reconnecting DAQiFi device..."
   644→powershell.exe -Command "usbipd attach --wsl --busid 2-4"
   645→sleep 2
   646→if [ -e /dev/ttyACM0 ]; then
   647→    echo "Device connected at /dev/ttyACM0"
   648→else
   649→    echo "Device not found, checking USB..."
   650→    lsusb | grep -i "04d8"
   651→fi
   652→```
   653→
   654→#### WiFi Configuration Test Sequence
   655→1. **Power off**: `SYST:POW:STAT 0`
   656→2. **Configure SSID**: `SYST:COMM:LAN:SSID "TestSSID"`
   657→3. **Apply settings**: `SYST:COMM:LAN:APPLY`
   658→4. **Power on**: `SYST:POW:STAT 1`
   659→5. **Check networks**: `cmd.exe /c "netsh wlan show networks"`
   660→6. **Save settings**: `SYST:COMM:LAN:SAVE`
   661→
   662→#### Key Testing Insights
   663→- WiFi initialization takes ~2-3 seconds after power up
   664→- APPLY command needed to activate runtime settings
   665→- SAVE command needed to persist to NVM
   666→- Device may load saved NVM settings on power up, not runtime settings
   667→- Power states: 0=STANDBY, 1=POWERED_UP, 2=POWERED_UP_EXT_DOWN
   668→
   669→### Shell Script Wrapper for Device Commands
   670→
   671→To avoid permission prompts with complex piped commands, create temporary shell scripts:
   672→
   673→**IMPORTANT**: Always use the filename `/tmp/temp.sh` for test scripts. This allows permissions to persist once approved in `.claude/settings.local.json`.
   674→
   675→```bash
   676→#!/bin/bash
   677→# Always use: /tmp/temp.sh
   678→send_cmd() {
   679→    local cmd="$1"
   680→    local delay="${2:-0.5}"
   681→    (echo -e "${cmd}\r"; sleep $delay) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 2>&1 | tail -20
   682→}
   683→
   684→# Use the function
   685→send_cmd "SYST:POW:STAT 0" 1
   686→send_cmd "*IDN?"
   687→```
   688→
   689→Then execute with:
   690→```bash
   691→chmod +x /tmp/temp.sh
   692→dos2unix /tmp/temp.sh  # Fix line endings if needed
   693→/tmp/temp.sh
   694→```
   695→
   696→This approach:
   697→- Avoids complex inline piped commands that trigger prompts
   698→- Makes tests repeatable and easier to debug
   699→- Uses consistent filename `/tmp/temp.sh` for persistent permissions
   700→- Add to `.claude/settings.local.json` permissions: `"Bash(/tmp/temp.sh:*)"`
   701→
   702→### Git Configuration
   703→- Ignore line ending changes when reviewing diffs (Windows/Linux compatibility)
   704→- ALWAYS test changes on hardware before committing
   705→- Use descriptive commit messages that explain the problem and solution
   706→
   707→### Commit Message Format
   708→
   709→This project uses **Conventional Commits** format for all commit messages:
   710→
   711→```
   712→<type>(<scope>): <subject>
   713→
   714→<body>
   715→
   716→<footer>
   717→```
   718→
   719→#### Types
   720→- `feat`: New feature
   721→- `fix`: Bug fix
   722→- `docs`: Documentation only changes
   723→- `style`: Changes that don't affect code meaning (white-space, formatting)
   724→- `refactor`: Code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature
   725→- `perf`: Performance improvement
   726→- `test`: Adding missing tests or correcting existing tests
   727→- `build`: Changes to build system or dependencies
   728→- `ci`: Changes to CI configuration files and scripts
   729→- `chore`: Other changes that don't modify src or test files
   730→
   731→#### Examples
   732→```
   733→fix(power): enable BQ24297 OTG mode for battery operation
   734→
   735→The device was powering off when USB disconnected due to insufficient
   736→voltage for the 3.3V regulator. OTG boost mode provides 5V from battery.
   737→
   738→Fixes #23
   739→```
   740→
   741→```
   742→feat(scpi): add battery diagnostics to SYST:INFO command
   743→
   744→Added comprehensive battery status information including charge state,
   745→voltage, NTC status, and power-up readiness.
   746→```
   747→
   748→#### Guidelines
   749→- Use present tense ("add" not "added")
   750→- Use imperative mood ("fix" not "fixes" or "fixed")
   751→- First line should be 72 characters or less
   752→- Reference issues and PRs in the footer
   753→- Breaking changes should be noted with `BREAKING CHANGE:` in the footer
   754→
   755→## Continuous Testing and Automation
   756→
   757→### Setting Up for Unprompted Testing
   758→
   759→1. **Permissions Configuration** (`.claude/settings.local.json`):
   760→   ```json
   761→   {
   762→     "permissions": {
   763→       "allow": [
   764→         "Bash(*)"
   765→       ],
   766→       "deny": []
   767→     }
   768→   }
   769→   ```
   770→   - Use wildcard `Bash(*)` for unrestricted testing
   771→   - Avoids interruption prompts during test execution
   772→   - Can be more restrictive for production use
   773→
   774→2. **Device Connection from WSL**:
   775→   ```bash
   776→   # List USB devices
   777→   powershell.exe -Command "usbipd list"
   778→   
   779→   # Attach DAQiFi device (usually 2-4)
   780→   powershell.exe -Command "usbipd attach --wsl --busid 2-4"
   781→   
   782→   # Verify connection
   783→   ls -la /dev/ttyACM0
   784→   ```
   785→
   786→3. **Programming Device from WSL**:
   787→   ```bash
   788→   # Using Windows IPE (more reliable than WSL passthrough)
   789→   cmd.exe /c "C:\"Program Files\"\\Microchip\\MPLABX\\v6.25\\mplab_platform\\mplab_ipe\\ipecmd.exe -P32MZ2048EFM144 -TPPK4 -F\"C:\\path\\to\\firmware.hex\" -M -OL"
   790→   
   791→   # IMPORTANT: After programming, always reattach the device to WSL
   792→   powershell.exe -Command "usbipd attach --wsl --busid 2-4"
   793→   ```
   794→
   795→### Real-Time Testing Guidelines
   796→
   797→1. **Avoid Echo Output to Windows**:
   798→   - Don't echo test results - Claude should observe and report internally
   799→   - Reduces prompting issues and improves test speed
   800→   - Example: Instead of `echo "Test passed"`, observe results and summarize
   801→
   802→2. **Efficient Test Commands**:
   803→   ```bash
   804→   # Good: Direct picocom command with minimal output
   805→   (echo -e "*IDN?\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 2>&1 | tail -5
   806→   
   807→   # Avoid: Multiple echo statements that trigger prompts
   808→   ```
   809→
   810→3. **State Management for Testing**:
   811→   - Always ensure device is in known state before tests
   812→   - Power cycle if needed: `SYST:POW:STAT 0` then `SYST:POW:STAT 1`
   813→   - Clear error queue: `SYST:ERR?` until "No error"
   814→   - Abort ongoing operations: `ABOR`
   815→
   816→4. **Test Automation Scripts**:
   817→   - Create self-contained test scripts that manage state
   818→   - Include setup, test, and cleanup phases
   819→   - Return clear pass/fail status
   820→   - Log results to files for post-analysis
   821→
   822→### Testing Best Practices
   823→
   824→1. **Device State Verification**:
   825→   ```bash
   826→   # Check device ready
   827→   *IDN?  # Should return DAQiFi info
   828→   
   829→   # Check power state
   830→   SYST:POW:STAT?  # 0=STANDBY, 1=POWERED_UP, 2=POWERED_UP_EXT_DOWN
   831→   
   832→   # Check errors
   833→   SYST:ERR?  # Should be "No error"
   834→   ```
   835→
   836→2. **Common Testing Patterns**:
   837→   - Input filtering: Send dangerous characters, verify they're filtered
   838→   - Command execution: Send valid commands, verify responses
   839→   - Error handling: Send invalid commands, check error messages
   840→   - State persistence: Change settings, power cycle, verify retained
   841→
   842→3. **Automated Test Execution**:
   843→   - Use bash scripts with proper error handling
   844→   - Capture output for analysis without displaying
   845→   - Report summary results only
   846→   - Example structure:
   847→     ```bash
   848→     #!/bin/bash
   849→     # Setup
   850→     device_setup() { ... }
   851→     
   852→     # Tests
   853→     test_feature() { 
   854→         result=$(send_command "...")
   855→         [[ "$result" == "expected" ]] && return 0 || return 1
   856→     }
   857→     
   858→     # Run tests and report
   859→     device_setup
   860→     test_feature && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"
   861→     ```
   862→
   863→### Known Issues and Workarounds
   864→
   865→1. **USBPcap Filter**: Interferes with device passthrough
   866→   - Use `--force` flag if needed
   867→   - Or program from Windows side directly
   868→
   869→2. **Line Ending Issues**: Windows creates CRLF in scripts
   870→   - Fix with: `dos2unix script.sh` or use `sed -i 's/\r$//'`
   871→   - Or create scripts directly in WSL
   872→
   873→3. **Permission Prompts**: Even with wildcards, complex commands may prompt
   874→   - Keep commands simple
   875→   - Use script files instead of complex one-liners
   876→   - Batch related commands together
   877→- pic32 tris convention is 1=input and 0=output
   878→- when implementing new code/features remember that we have multiple configurations so we need to ensure we are keeping all the structs consistant as well as the handling functions, etc.
   879→- always verify scpi command syntax - don't guess.
   880→- we don't need to generate analysis docs or the like unless asked
   881→- all errors should go through the error logging function and doesn't need to be sent out in the stream at all. if there is an
   882→ error, the SCPI command should return the error through its own handling. then the user calls SYSTem:LOG? to know what the
   883→error was.
   884→- all errors should go through the error logging function and doesn't need to be sent out in the stream at all. if there is an
   885→ error, the SCPI command should return the error through its own handling. then the user calls SYSTem:LOG? to know what the
   886→error was.

content.content:
1→# Project Memory
     2→
     3→## BQ24297 Power Management
     4→
     5→### OTG Pin and DPDM Detection (PR #186)
     6→- BQ24297 DPDM detection uses OTG pin state to set IINLIM: OTG LOW = 100mA, OTG HIGH = 500mA (datasheet Table 9-1)
     7→- With OTG LOW + good battery, chip enters HIZ mode (REG00[7]=1), drawing <30uA from VBUS — blocks all USB input current
     8→- Fix: Set OTG GPIO as input (BATT_MAN_OTG_InputEnable) — hardware pullup holds HIGH
     9→- OTG pin on RK5 has hardware pullup on the board
    10→- BQ24297 **always** runs DPDM detection when new DC source plugs in, regardless of REG07[7] setting
    11→- BQ24297 resets IINLIM on new source detection — writing IINLIM before VBUS present gets overwritten
    12→- USB attach delay increased to 1000ms (pdMS_TO_TICKS) to allow DPDM detection to complete
    13→- Added VBUS re-check before USB_DEVICE_Attach after delay to handle removal during wait
    14→
    15→### BQ24297 Register Init (Config_Settings)
    16→- REG00: Clear HIZ, VINDPM=3.88V min, preserve IINLIM
    17→- REG01: OTG=0, CHG=1, SYS_MIN=3.0V, watchdog reset
    18→- REG02: Fast charge 2000mA
    19→- REG03: Pre-charge 128mA, termination 128mA
    20→- REG04: Charge voltage 4.096V, BATLOWV=3.0V
    21→- REG05: Watchdog disabled, charge timer 20hr
    22→- REG06: Boost 4.998V, thermal 120°C
    23→- REG07: Ensure BATFET enabled (bit 5=0)
    24→- AutoSetILim sets IINLIM to 2000mA for all vBusStat cases
    25→
    26→### BYPASS_BQ24297
    27→- Defined in BQ24297.c (commented out by default)
    28→- Skips I2C open, returns fake register values, fakes good status
    29→- Board needs BQ24297 register init to pass power — can't just skip all I2C
    30→- But BYPASS_BQ24297 works because it fakes good status values for power state machine
    31→
    32→### Key Learnings
    33→- PIC32 TRIS: 1=input, 0=output
    34→- USB descriptor bMaxPower is value × 2mA (250 = 500mA)
    35→- USB self-powered attribute + max power coexist fine
    36→- BQ24297 datasheet at `datasheets/bq24297.pdf`
    37→- PDF reading requires poppler-utils installed
    38→

content.content:
case USB_DEVICE_EVENT_POWER_DETECTED:
 
-            /* VBUS was detected. Wait 1000ms for BQ24297 DPDM detection to complete before attaching USB device */
+            /* VBUS was detected. This callback runs in the USB device task context,
+             * so vTaskDelay yields CPU to other tasks (does not block the system).
+             *
+             * 100ms delay: lets the RC low-pass filter on the VBUS sense line
+             * settle before we attach. Previously 1000ms to wait for BQ24297
+             * DPDM detection; reduced now that ManageIINLIM handles IINLIM
+             * independently via I2C.
+             *

+ * Command: SYSTem:POWer:BQ:ILIM <value>
+ * Command: SYSTem:POWer:BQ:DPDM
+ * Command: SYSTem:POWer:BQ:DIAGnostics?
     {.pattern = "SYSTem:POWer:STATe", .callback = SCPI_SetPowerState,},
     {.pattern = "SYSTem:POWer:AUTO:EXTernal?", .callback = SCPI_GetAutoExtPower,},
     {.pattern = "SYSTem:POWer:AUTO:EXTernal", .callback = SCPI_SetAutoExtPower,},
+    {.pattern = "SYSTem:POWer:BQ:REGisters?", .callback = SCPI_GetBQRegisters,},
+    {.pattern = "SYSTem:POWer:BQ:ILIM", .callback = SCPI_SetBQILim,},
+    {.pattern = "SYSTem:POWer:BQ:DPDM", .callback = SCPI_ForceDPDM,},
+    {.pattern = "SYSTem:POWer:BQ:DIAGnostics?", .callback = SCPI_GetBQDiagnostics,},

content.content:
1→# Project Memory
     2→
     3→## BQ24297 Power Management
     4→
     5→### OTG Pin and DPDM Detection (PR #186)
     6→- BQ24297 DPDM detection uses OTG pin state to set IINLIM: OTG LOW = 100mA, OTG HIGH = 500mA (datasheet Table 9-1)
     7→- With OTG LOW + good battery, chip enters HIZ mode (REG00[7]=1), drawing <30uA from VBUS — blocks all USB input current
     8→- Fix: Set OTG GPIO as input (BATT_MAN_OTG_InputEnable) — hardware pullup holds HIGH
     9→- OTG pin on RK5 has hardware pullup on the board
    10→- BQ24297 **always** runs DPDM detection when new DC source plugs in, regardless of REG07[7] setting
    11→- BQ24297 resets IINLIM on new source detection — writing IINLIM before VBUS present gets overwritten
    12→- USB attach delay increased to 1000ms (pdMS_TO_TICKS) to allow DPDM detection to complete
    13→- Added VBUS re-check before USB_DEVICE_Attach after delay to handle removal during wait
    14→
    15→### BQ24297 Register Init (Config_Settings)
    16→- REG00: Clear HIZ, VINDPM=3.88V min, preserve IINLIM
    17→- REG01: OTG=0, CHG=1, SYS_MIN=3.0V, watchdog reset
    18→- REG02: Fast charge 2000mA
    19→- REG03: Pre-charge 128mA, termination 128mA
    20→- REG04: Charge voltage 4.096V, BATLOWV=3.0V
    21→- REG05: Watchdog disabled, charge timer 20hr
    22→- REG06: Boost 4.998V, thermal 120°C
    23→- REG07: Ensure BATFET enabled (bit 5=0)
    24→- AutoSetILim sets IINLIM to 2000mA for all vBusStat cases
    25→
    26→### BYPASS_BQ24297
    27→- Defined in BQ24297.c (commented out by default)
    28→- Skips I2C open, returns fake register values, fakes good status
    29→- Board needs BQ24297 register init to pass power — can't just skip all I2C
    30→- But BYPASS_BQ24297 works because it fakes good status values for power state machine
    31→
    32→### Key Learnings
    33→- PIC32 TRIS: 1=input, 0=output
    34→- USB descriptor bMaxPower is value × 2mA (250 = 500mA)
    35→- USB self-powered attribute + max power coexist fine
    36→- BQ24297 datasheet at `datasheets/bq24297.pdf`
    37→- PDF reading requires poppler-utils installed
    38→

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of all the changes. Let me compile my findings:

## Report: Documentation Update Needs for Timer-Based IINLIM PR

Based on analysis of the git diff from `main..HEAD`, here are the sections of CLAUDE.md and MEMORY.md that need updating:

### MEMORY.md Updates Needed

**Section: "OTG Pin and DPDM Detection (PR #186)"** (lines 5-13)

This section is now **stale and needs complete replacement**. Key changes:
1. **OTG pin behavior reversed**: Was "Set as input with hardware pullup", now "Drive LOW via output"
2. **DPDM responsibility shifted**: No longer relies on OTG pin state during DPDM; BQ24297 always uses its internal DPDM detection
3. **IINLIM management moved**: Now handled by `BQ24297_ManageIINLIM()` state machine instead of old `BQ24297_AutoSetILim()`
4. **USB attach delay reduced**: From 1000ms to 100ms (now just for RC filter settling, not DPDM wait)
5. **New state machine**: IDLE → WAIT_DPDM (1s) → WAIT_USB (2s) → SETTLED

**What the updated section should document:**
- OTG GPIO driven LOW to set conservative 100mA initial IINLIM
- DPDM detection runs automatically (1s wait for hardware to settle)
- Timer-based state machine runs periodically from `Power_Tasks()`
- After DPDM completes, waits 2 seconds to check `UsbCdc_IsConfigured()`
- Sets final IINLIM: 500mA for USB host, 2000mA for wall charger
- VBUS edge detection triggers state transitions

**Also update the "BQ24297 Register Init (Config_Settings)" section** (lines 15-24):
- Remove note about `BQ24297_AutoSetILim()`
- Add that initial IINLIM is set to 500mA in Config_Settings
- Note that `BQ24297_ManageIINLIM()` overrides this based on USB enumeration

---

### CLAUDE.md Updates Needed

**1. Section: "Power Management (BQ24297)"** (lines 79) in Component Hierarchy
- Currently says "Power Management (BQ24297)" with no detail
- Should add: "BQ24297 with timer-based IINLIM state machine" or reference the new SCPI diagnostic commands

**2. Add new section: "BQ24297 IINLIM Management"** (suggest after DAC7718 section, around line 350)

Should document:
- **OTG Pin Configuration**: Driven LOW for conservative 100mA startup
- **State Machine Overview**: IDLE → WAIT_DPDM → WAIT_USB → SETTLED
- **Timing**: 1000ms for DPDM completion, 2000ms for USB enumeration check
- **Final Limits**: 500mA (USB host) vs 2000mA (wall charger)
- **Calling Convention**: `Power_Tasks()` calls `BQ24297_ManageIINLIM()` with VBUS level
- **USB Enumeration Detection**: Uses `UsbCdc_IsConfigured()` flag

**3. Add section: "SCPI Diagnostic Commands for BQ24297"** (around line 314, before or after DAC SCPI commands)

Should document these new commands:
```
SYST:POW:BQ:REGisters?    # Read all BQ24297 registers (REG00-REG0A hex)
SYST:POW:BQ:ILIM <value>  # Set IINLIM directly (0-7, enum: 100mA, 150mA, 500mA, 900mA, 1A, 1.5A, 2A, 3A)
SYST:POW:BQ:DPDM          # Force DPDM re-detection (triggers falling edge to IDLE → WAIT_DPDM)
SYST:POW:BQ:DIAGnostics?  # Get state machine diagnostics (current state, timestamp, VBUS status)
```

---

### New Information for MEMORY.md

Add a new section to document the timer-based approach:

**Key architectural insight**: The PR shifts from passive (OTG pin high during DPDM) to active (timer-based monitoring with I2C writes). This is safer because:
1. BQ24297 hardware DPDM runs regardless of OTG pin state
2. We now wait for DPDM to actually complete (REG07 bit 7) before writing IINLIM
3. USB enumeration status drives final decision (500mA vs 2000mA)

---

### Files with Stale Content

1. **MEMORY.md** - PR #186 description is superseded
2. **CLAUDE.md** - Power management section in Architecture lacks detail on new IINLIM management
3. **CLAUDE.md** - SCPI command table (lines 106-149) should reference new diagnostic commands in a new subsection

---

### Specific Line/Section References

| File | Location | Issue | Priority |
|------|----------|-------|----------|
| MEMORY.md | Lines 5-13 | "OTG Pin and DPDM Detection" completely stale | HIGH |
| MEMORY.md | Lines 15-24 | "BQ24297 Register Init" needs update on AutoSetILim removal | HIGH |
| CLAUDE.md | Line 79 | "Power Management (BQ24297)" lacks architectural detail | MEDIUM |
| CLAUDE.md | After line 349 | Missing "IINLIM Management" section | HIGH |
| CLAUDE.md | After line 313 | Missing "BQ24297 Diagnostic SCPI Commands" section | HIGH |
| CLAUDE.md | Line 603, 557 | Testing notes mention 1000ms delay (now 100ms) | LOW |

---

### Additional Context

- **New functions**: `BQ24297_SetIINLIM()`, `BQ24297_ManageIINLIM()`
- **New UsbCdc function**: `UsbCdc_IsConfigured()` tracks USB SET_CONFIGURATION state
- **New SCPI functions**: 4 diagnostic commands for BQ24297 debugging
- **Timing change**: POWER_DETECTED delay reduced 1000ms → 100ms (affects Section 3 of testing notes in CLAUDE.md)
- **scpi_printf helper**: Added in SCPIInterface.c (not documented in CLAUDE.md, but low priority since it's internal)

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my findings. Based on my thorough review of all the code changes in the PR, here are the issues I found:

## Comment Accuracy Review - PR (feat/timer-based-iinlim)

### CRITICAL ISSUE: Contradictory Comments About OTG GPIO Configuration

**Location**: BQ24297.c lines 65-68, 82-85, 118-120 (CURRENT CODE)

**Problem**: The comments contradict the code AND contradict the project memory (MEMORY.md).

```c
// CURRENT COMMENT (WRONG):
// Drive OTG LOW — DPDM will set 100mA (conservative).
// ManageIINLIM state machine overrides IINLIM via I2C after DPDM completes.
BATT_MAN_OTG_OutputEnable();
BATT_MAN_OTG_Clear();
```

**What's Wrong**:
1. **Comment contradicts MEMORY.md**: MEMORY.md line 6 states "OTG LOW = 100mA, OTG HIGH = 500mA" but line 8 says "Fix: Set OTG GPIO as input (BATT_MAN_OTG_InputEnable) — hardware pullup holds HIGH". The fix (PR #186) was to set it as INPUT, not OUTPUT LOW.
2. **Architecture contradiction**: The old code (main branch) had `BATT_MAN_OTG_InputEnable()` which matches the fix in MEMORY.md. This new code drives it LOW (output), which is the opposite of the documented fix.
3. **Functionality issue**: Setting OTG as OUTPUT LOW deliberately drives it low, versus the previous design where it was INPUT with hardware pullup holding HIGH (500mA). The new code appears to revert to conservative 100mA detection.

**Expected Comment**:
```c
// Initialize OTG GPIO as input - hardware pullup holds HIGH
// OTG HIGH during DPDM detection = 500mA IINLIM (vs 100mA when LOW)
BATT_MAN_OTG_InputEnable();
```

### ISSUE 2: Misleading Comment in BQ24297_ManageIINLIM

**Location**: BQ24297.h lines 171-173

**Problem**: Enum comment says "waiting 2s for USB enumeration" but this is only ONE of several possible outcomes.

```c
IINLIM_STATE_WAIT_USB,       // DPDM done, IINLIM=500mA, waiting 2s for USB enumeration
```

**What's Wrong**: The comment is correct but incomplete. The state doesn't JUST wait for USB enumeration—after 2s it either:
- Sets 500mA if USB is configured
- Sets 2000mA if USB is NOT configured (wall charger detected)

**Suggested Improvement**:
```c
IINLIM_STATE_WAIT_USB,       // DPDM done, IINLIM=500mA, waiting 2s for USB enumeration check
```

Or:
```c
IINLIM_STATE_WAIT_USB,       // After DPDM, waiting 2s to determine USB vs charger
```

### ISSUE 3: Format String Errors in PowerApi.c (Minor but Real)

**Location**: firmware/src/HAL/Power/PowerApi.c lines 502, 532, 551, 558, 566

**Problem**: Format specifier changed from `%.1f` to `%u` but comments still reference floats.

```c
// Line 502:
LOG_D("Power_UpdateState: Battery at %u%%, transitioning...",   // Format = unsigned int
      pData->chargePct);

// Line 530:
LOG_D("Auto-recovery: Enabling external power - %s, battery at %u%%",  // Format = unsigned int
      hasExternalPower ? "..." : "...", pData->chargePct);
```

**Status**: Actually CORRECT in the code (not a mismatch). These are proper fixes changing from float formatting to unsigned int. No issue here—actually good cleanup.

### All Other Comments: CORRECT

Reviewed sections:
- ✅ BQ24297.h: doc comments on BQ24297_SetIINLIM and BQ24297_ManageIINLIM are accurate
- ✅ BQ24297.h: enum eIINLIM_State comments are accurate (see issue 2 caveat)
- ✅ BQ24297.c: Inline comments around ManageIINLIM state machine logic are accurate
- ✅ PowerApi.c: Comments on ManageIINLIM call are accurate (line 148-150)
- ✅ UsbCdc.c: Comments around POWER_DETECTED timing are accurate and well-documented (100ms delay)
- ✅ UsbCdc.h: Doc comments on isConfigured field and UsbCdc_IsConfigured() are accurate
- ✅ SCPIInterface.c: All doc comments on new SCPI callbacks are accurate and detailed
- ✅ SCPIInterface.h: Doc comment on scpi_printf is accurate

---

## Summary of Findings

| Issue | Severity | Location | Status |
|-------|----------|----------|--------|
| OTG GPIO configuration contradicts MEMORY.md and architecture | **CRITICAL** | BQ24297.c:65-68, 82-85, 118-120 | Needs clarification/fix |
| Enum comment incompleteness (WAIT_USB) | **Minor** | BQ24297.h:173 | Documentation improvement |
| Format string fixes in PowerApi.c | None | N/A | Code is correct |

The critical issue is that the new code drives OTG GPIO LOW (output mode) with comments saying this is the fix, but MEMORY.md documents the fix as setting it INPUT with hardware pullup HIGH. This architectural change needs clarification in comments or code verification.

3. Update CLAUDE.md with new SCPI commands and IINLIM management info
4. Create a test plan

The OTG comment flagged as a contradiction is actually intentional - PR #186 set OTG as input, but this PR changes it back to output LOW as part of the timer-based IINLIM approach. The BQ24297.c comments are correct for the new design, so I need to update MEMORY.md to match, then add the SCPI command documentation and test plan.

file.content:
# Project Memory

## BQ24297 Power Management

### OTG Pin and DPDM Detection (PR #186)
- BQ24297 DPDM detection uses OTG pin state to set IINLIM: OTG LOW = 100mA, OTG HIGH = 500mA (datasheet Table 9-1)
- With OTG LOW + good battery, chip enters HIZ mode (REG00[7]=1), drawing <30uA from VBUS — blocks all USB input current
- Fix: Set OTG GPIO as input (BATT_MAN_OTG_InputEnable) — hardware pullup holds HIGH
- OTG pin on RK5 has hardware pullup on the board
- BQ24297 **always** runs DPDM detection when new DC source plugs in, regardless of REG07[7] setting
- BQ24297 resets IINLIM on new source detection — writing IINLIM before VBUS present gets overwritten
- USB attach delay increased to 1000ms (pdMS_TO_TICKS) to allow DPDM detection to complete
- Added VBUS re-check before USB_DEVICE_Attach after delay to handle removal during wait

### BQ24297 Register Init (Config_Settings)
- REG00: Clear HIZ, VINDPM=3.88V min, preserve IINLIM
- REG01: OTG=0, CHG=1, SYS_MIN=3.0V, watchdog reset
- REG02: Fast charge 2000mA
- REG03: Pre-charge 128mA, termination 128mA
- REG04: Charge voltage 4.096V, BATLOWV=3.0V
- REG05: Watchdog disabled, charge timer 20hr
- REG06: Boost 4.998V, thermal 120°C
- REG07: Ensure BATFET enabled (bit 5=0)
- AutoSetILim sets IINLIM to 2000mA for all vBusStat cases

### BYPASS_BQ24297
- Defined in BQ24297.c (commented out by default)
- Skips I2C open, returns fake register values, fakes good status
- Board needs BQ24297 register init to pass power — can't just skip all I2C
- But BYPASS_BQ24297 works because it fakes good status values for power state machine

### Key Learnings
- PIC32 TRIS: 1=input, 0=output
- USB descriptor bMaxPower is value × 2mA (250 = 500mA)
- USB self-powered attribute + max power coexist fine
- BQ24297 datasheet at `datasheets/bq24297.pdf`
- PDF reading requires poppler-utils installed

file.content:
2. **Power State Required**: Always power up the device before attempting WiFi or DAC operations
   ```bash
   # Check power state (0=STANDBY, 1=POWERED_UP, 2=POWERED_UP_EXT_DOWN)
   (echo -e "SYST:POW:STAT?\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 | tail -5
   
   # Power up device for full functionality (enables 10V rail needed for DAC7718)
   (echo -e "SYST:POW:STAT 1\r"; sleep 1) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 | tail-5
   ```

3. **Use Exact SCPI Commands**: Don't guess command syntax. Check the actual command table in `SCPIInterface.c`
   ```bash
   # Correct commands (from SCPIInterface.c):
   SYST:COMM:LAN:SSIDSTR?    # Signal strength (not SSID:STR)
   SYST:COMM:LAN:NETTYPE?     # Network type (not MODE or NETWORK)
   SYST:COMM:LAN:ADDR?        # IP address (not ADDRESS or IP)
   ```

#### USB Device Access from WSL
1. **Initial device attachment** (from Windows PowerShell as admin):
   ```powershell
   usbipd list
   # Find DAQiFi device (e.g., "USB Serial Device (COM3)")
   usbipd attach --wsl --busid <BUSID>
   ```
   Note: May require `--force` if USBPcap filter is installed

2. **Reconnecting device from WSL side** (after PC sleep or device reprogramming):
   ```bash
   # List USB devices to find DAQiFi
   powershell.exe -Command "usbipd list"
   # Look for: 2-4    04d8:f794  USB Serial Device (COM3)
   
   # Attach from WSL
   powershell.exe -Command "usbipd attach --wsl --busid 2-4"
   
   # Wait for device to appear
   sleep 2 && ls -la /dev/ttyACM*
   ```

3. **Verify device in WSL**:
   ```bash
   lsusb | grep -i "04d8"
   # Should show: Microchip Technology, Inc. Nyquist
   
   ls -la /dev/ttyACM*
   # Should show: /dev/ttyACM0
   ```

#### Sending SCPI Commands
Use picocom for reliable serial communication:
```bash
# Query device identification
(echo -e "*IDN?\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 2>&1 | tail -20

# Common SCPI commands:
# WiFi configuration
(echo -e "SYST:COMM:LAN:SSID?\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 2>&1 | tail -20
(echo -e "SYST:COMM:LAN:SSID \"NetworkName\"\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 2>&1 | tail -20

# Power management
(echo -e "SYST:POW:STAT?\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 2>&1 | tail -20
(echo -e "SYST:POW:STAT 2\r"; sleep 0.5) | picocom -b 115200 -q -x 1000 /dev/ttyACM0 2>&1 | tail -20
```

Power states:
- 0: STANDBY (MCU on if USB powered, off if battery powered)
- 1: POWERED_UP (Board fully powered, all modules active)
- 2: POWERED_UP_EXT_DOWN (Partial power, external power disabled - low battery mode)

### OTG Pin and DPDM Detection (PR #186)
- BQ24297 DPDM detection uses OTG pin state to set IINLIM: OTG LOW = 100mA, OTG HIGH = 500mA (datasheet Table 9-1)
- With OTG LOW + good battery, chip enters HIZ mode (REG00[7]=1), drawing <30uA from VBUS — blocks all USB input current
- Fix: Set OTG GPIO as input (BATT_MAN_OTG_InputEnable) — hardware pullup holds HIGH
- OTG pin on RK5 has hardware pullup on the board
- BQ24297 **always** runs DPDM detection when new DC source plugs in, regardless of REG07[7] setting
- BQ24297 resets IINLIM on new source detection — writing IINLIM before VBUS present gets overwritten
- USB attach delay increased to 1000ms (pdMS_TO_TICKS) to allow DPDM detection to complete
- Added VBUS re-check before USB_DEVICE_Attach after delay to handle removal during wait

### BQ24297 Register Init (Config_Settings)
- REG00: Clear HIZ, VINDPM=3.88V min, preserve IINLIM
- REG01: OTG=0, CHG=1, SYS_MIN=3.0V, watchdog reset
- REG02: Fast charge 2000mA
- REG03: Pre-charge 128mA, termination 128mA
- REG04: Charge voltage 4.096V, BATLOWV=3.0V
- REG05: Watchdog disabled, charge timer 20hr
- REG06: Boost 4.998V, thermal 120°C
- REG07: Ensure BATFET enabled (bit 5=0)
- AutoSetILim sets IINLIM to 2000mA for all vBusStat cases
input.new_string:
### IINLIM Management (PR #189 — replaces PR #186 approach)
- OTG pin driven LOW (output) so DPDM starts at 100mA (conservative)
- Timer-based state machine `BQ24297_ManageIINLIM()` runs from `Power_Tasks()` (~100ms interval)
- State machine: IDLE → WAIT_DPDM (1s min, 3s timeout) → WAIT_USB (2s) → SETTLED
- After DPDM completes, sets 500mA and checks `UsbCdc_IsConfigured()` over 2s window
- USB host enumerated → keeps 500mA; no enumeration (wall charger) → bumps to 2000mA
- VBUS loss in any state resets to IDLE with 500mA
- VBUS presence threshold: `>= USBHS_VBUS_BELOW_VBUSVALID` (~4V) to handle wall charger sag
- USB attach delay: 100ms (RC filter settle only, IINLIM handled independently)
- BQ24297 **always** runs DPDM on new DC source, regardless of REG07[7]
- `UsbCdc_IsConfigured()` tracks USB SET_CONFIGURATION event; cleared on RESET, DECONFIGURED, POWER_REMOVED, ERROR

### BQ24297 Register Init (Config_Settings)
- REG00: Clear HIZ, VINDPM=3.88V min, preserve IINLIM
- REG01: OTG=0, CHG=1, SYS_MIN=3.0V, watchdog reset
- REG02: Fast charge 2000mA
- REG03: Pre-charge 128mA, termination 128mA
- REG04: Charge voltage 4.096V, BATLOWV=3.0V
- REG05: Watchdog disabled, charge timer 20hr
- REG06: Boost 4.998V, thermal 120°C
- REG07: Ensure BATFET enabled (bit 5=0)
- Initial IINLIM set to 500mA; ManageIINLIM state machine adjusts based on USB enumeration

### SCPI Diagnostic Commands (PR #189)
- `SYST:POW:BQ:REG?` — dump all BQ24297 registers (REG00-REG0A hex)
- `SYST:POW:BQ:ILIM <0-7>` — set IINLIM directly (with readback verify)
- `SYST:POW:BQ:DPDM` — force DPDM re-detection, polls until complete
- `SYST:POW:BQ:DIAG?` — comprehensive diagnostics (battery, registers, GPIO, IINLIM state, power state)

-- USB attach delay increased to 1000ms (pdMS_TO_TICKS) to allow DPDM detection to complete
-- Added VBUS re-check before USB_DEVICE_Attach after delay to handle removal during wait
+- OTG pin driven LOW (output) so DPDM starts at 100mA (conservative)
+- Timer-based state machine `BQ24297_ManageIINLIM()` runs from `Power_Tasks()` (~100ms interval)
+- State machine: IDLE → WAIT_DPDM (1s min, 3s timeout) → WAIT_USB (2s) → SETTLED
+- After DPDM completes, sets 500mA and checks `UsbCdc_IsConfigured()` over 2s window
+- USB host enumerated → keeps 500mA; no enumeration (wall charger) → bumps to 2000mA
+- VBUS loss in any state resets to IDLE with 500mA
+- VBUS presence threshold: `>= USBHS_VBUS_BELOW_VBUSVALID` (~4V) to handle wall charger sag
+- USB attach delay: 100ms (RC filter settle only, IINLIM handled independently)
+- BQ24297 **always** runs DPDM on new DC source, regardless of REG07[7]
+- `UsbCdc_IsConfigured()` tracks USB SET_CONFIGURATION event; cleared on RESET, DECONFIGURED, POWER_REMOVED, ERROR
- REG00: Clear HIZ, VINDPM=3.88V min, preserve IINLIM
-- AutoSetILim sets IINLIM to 2000mA for all vBusStat cases
+- Initial IINLIM set to 500mA; ManageIINLIM state machine adjusts based on USB enumeration
+- `SYST:POW:BQ:DPDM` — force DPDM re-detection, polls until complete
+- `SYST:POW:BQ:DIAG?` — comprehensive diagnostics (battery, registers, GPIO, IINLIM state, power state)
- Defined in BQ24297.c (commented out by default)
- Skips I2C open, returns fake register values, fakes good status
